prompt:
Hey -- research ops here. We keep renting SaaS survey tools that can't do the one thing we need (branching), so I got budget for an in-house CLI. Python 3.12, stdlib only, state in one JSON db file. Please keep three module names stable because the intranet form renderer will import them: `cli.py` (commands), `survey.py` (branching + validation, pure functions, no file IO), `store.py` (owns the db format). More modules are fine.

A survey definition is a JSON file we author by hand: `{"title": ..., "questions": [...]}` where each question has `id`, `text`, `type` of `choice` (with `options`), `number` (optional integer `min`/`max`, inclusive), or `text`. Questions are required unless `"required": false`. Branching is `"show_if": {"other_qid": "value"}` -- the question only appears when that earlier question has exactly that answer, and this CHAINS: if the question you depend on is itself hidden, you're hidden too. At `define` time reject garbage definitions outright (nonzero exit, no db file created): duplicate ids, show_if pointing at an unknown or LATER question, a choice without options, an unknown type.

Commands, each its own process, all `python3 cli.py --db PATH ...`:
- `define DEFN_FILE` -- initialize the db from a definition. Db already exists: nonzero, file untouched (people WILL try to redefine mid-collection). Any other command before define: nonzero.
- `submit --id RESPONDENT q1=val q2=val ...` -- one whole response. Validation is the heart of it: every visible required question answered; answers to hidden questions REJECTED (that's how we catch bots replaying old forms); choice answers in the options; numbers are integers within min/max; unknown question ids rejected; args without `=` rejected; duplicate respondent id rejected (mention it). Any problem: nonzero exit naming the offending question id on stderr, db untouched.
- `tally` -- choice questions only, definition order, options in definition order, one line each, exactly `QID<TAB>OPTION<TAB>COUNT<TAB>PCT%` where PCT has one decimal (like `66.7%` or `0.0%`) and the denominator is the number of respondents who were actually SHOWN that question -- NOT total respondents; skew from lumping in people who never saw the question is exactly the bug we're escaping. Question nobody ever saw: count 0, `0.0%`, no crash.
- `export` -- responses as CSV on stdout: header `respondent,<qid...>` in definition order, one row per response in submission order, blank cell for hidden/unanswered, and real CSV quoting (free-text answers contain commas and quotes; only quote when needed).

I also want the pure API testable on its own: `survey.visible_questions(defn, answers) -> [qid, ...]` (definition order) and `survey.validate(defn, answers) -> list of problem strings` (empty list means fine, each problem names its question id).

Acceptance contract is `python3 test_survey.py` -- leave the test file alone.
